_doc: Template: run a trained policy WITHOUT saving data. Pure live inference for demos, latency benchmarks, or quick eyeballing of a checkpoint. Identical to infer_record except mode=infer and video defaults off — no dataset is written. Underscore-prefixed keys (_doc, _comment_*) are inline annotations stripped by the validator.

hardware_meta._comment_cameras: Same camera layout the policy was trained on. index_or_path: int → /dev/videoN, str → arbitrary path.

collection_meta._comment_operator_name: Whoever is supervising — even infer-only runs are tagged with a name.
collection_meta._comment_acting_arms: Which arm(s) the policy drives. Options: ['left', 'right'], ['left'], or ['right']. Match the policy's training scope.

task_description: FILL_ME_IN — what scene the policy is being demo'd on.

operator.prompt: FILL_ME_IN — the imperative the policy was trained on.
operator._comment_prompt: The initial prompt sent to the policy server. Stays active until you press a Ctrl+<key> shortcut from the 'prompts' block below.

policy_server._comment_policy_server: host: 'localhost' or LAN IP; port: typically 8000 / 8001. Required for any infer mode.

prompts._comment_prompts: Optional. Live prompt-switching map: pressing Ctrl+<key> during the session swaps the policy's prompt to the matching string starting on the next chunk. Keys MUST be a single character (digit or letter); multi-char keys are rejected at load. Omit the block (or leave it empty) to disable live switching. The switch is logged in real time; in modes that write a dataset (infer_record / self_play) the timeline lands in each episode's metadata under 'prompt_switches' and the active prompt is recorded per frame in the dataset's 'task' column. Common patterns: (1) variants on the same task — pick whichever phrasing the policy responds to best; (2) probing different sub-skills against a single checkpoint; (3) staging — start with a 'find' prompt, switch to 'grasp' once the object is in view.
prompts.1: FILL_ME_IN — first variant. Usually mirrors roles.operator.prompt so Ctrl+1 returns to the default.
prompts.3: FILL_ME_IN — third variant. Add or drop entries freely.

recording._comment_recording: Most fields here are vestigial in pure infer mode (no dataset is written). They're still type-checked, so leave the defaults unless you have a reason.
recording._comment_num_episodes: How many demo rollouts before the loop stops.
recording._comment_video: false by default in infer mode — no dataset means no video to encode.

inference._comment_inference: Same knobs as infer_record. See lerobot/recording/task/session_config.py:INFERENCE_DEFAULTS for defaults; the values below match those defaults plus a note per field.
inference._comment_model_name: Free-form checkpoint label (e.g. 'seatbelt_recap_v1'). Lands in meta.info.infer_meta. Use "" if you really have no name for it.
inference._comment_action_horizon: Must match the policy's training value.
inference._comment_persist_inference_log: No-op in pure infer mode — there's no dataset to colocate the log with. Listed here for parity; flip on in templates/infer_record.json or templates/self_play.json instead.
